examples.name: diversity_keeps_lower_scoring_story
examples.scenario: If three war-topic clusters land within a few points of each other, the service can reject one under the topic soft cap and keep a slightly lower-scoring economy cluster for diversity.

examples.name: low_score_story_rejected
examples.scenario: A commentary-like cluster below the editorial threshold is rejected even when capacity remains.

examples.name: geography_preference_breaks_near_tie
examples.scenario: Two near-tie clusters are both selectable, and the user preference leans toward national coverage, so the national story is preferred over an otherwise similar international story.

examples.name: domain_preference_breaks_near_tie
examples.scenario: Two near-tie clusters remain after higher-scoring stories, and the user preference leans toward sport, so the sport cluster is preferred over a close-scoring politics cluster.

examples.name: stronger_story_still_wins_over_preference
examples.scenario: A clearly stronger cluster stays selected even when user preferences favor another domain, because preferences only act as soft near-tie signals.
